input.text: Dr. Patel was so gentle with my extraction and explained every step before doing it. The billing department is another story — I was quoted one price and charged another, and nobody can tell me why. Also the parking lot is tiny, I circled for ten minutes. Overall a mixed bag honestly.

excerpts.text: Dr. Patel was so gentle with my extraction and explained every step before doing it.
I was quoted one price and charged another, and nobody can tell me why.
the parking lot is tiny, I circled for ten minutes.

notes: Canonical multi-topic case: provider care + billing + parking, one excerpt each. The closing filler sentence ('Overall a mixed bag honestly.') carries no aspect and must NOT become an excerpt. The billing excerpt starts mid-sentence — a clause is fine when it is self-contained — and every span must be verbatim at the stated offset.

input.text: the hygenist Maria was AMAZING she remembered my kids names and made my daughter laugh the whole time. front desk is a different experience tho... waited 25 min past my appt time and no one even said anything. would still come back for Maria alone

excerpts.text: the hygenist Maria was AMAZING she remembered my kids names and made my daughter laugh the whole time.
waited 25 min past my appt time and no one even said anything.
would still come back for Maria alone

notes: Typos and lowercase are the trap: 'hygenist', 'tho', missing apostrophes must be copied verbatim — a model that fixes spelling fails the substring gate. The wait-time excerpt drops the unquotable lead-in ('front desk is a different experience tho...') in favor of the self-contained clause after it.

input.text: I have severe dental anxiety and had been putting off a root canal for over a year. The whole team took it seriously: they walked me through the sedation options, checked in constantly, and gave me breaks whenever I raised my hand. I never once felt rushed or judged. If you are scared of the dentist, this is the practice to try.

excerpts.text: I have severe dental anxiety and had been putting off a root canal for over a year. The whole team took it seriously: they walked me through the sedation options, checked in constantly, and gave me breaks whenever I raised my hand. I never once felt rushed or judged. If you are scared of the dentist, this is the practice to try.

notes: One topic, many sentences: anxiety accommodation is the single aspect, so the whole text is one excerpt — splitting it would strand sentences that only work together. Guards against over-segmentation.

input.text: Dr. Ramos took the time to explain my treatment options without any pressure. The only downside was sitting in the waiting room for almost an hour past my appointment time.

excerpts.text: Dr. Ramos took the time to explain my treatment options without any pressure.
sitting in the waiting room for almost an hour past my appointment time

notes: Two clean aspects. The wait-time excerpt drops the unquotable lead-in ('The only downside was') in favor of the self-contained clause; token-overlap scoring tolerates a model that keeps the full sentence.

input.text: Dr. Chen was thorough and caught a problem two other dentists missed. The front desk checked me in within a minute of walking through the door. They gave me a written cost estimate before doing anything, which I appreciated. The office itself is spotless and modern.

excerpts.text: Dr. Chen was thorough and caught a problem two other dentists missed.
The front desk checked me in within a minute of walking through the door.
They gave me a written cost estimate before doing anything, which I appreciated.
The office itself is spotless and modern.

notes: Four distinct aspects, one sentence each — tests that the model neither merges aspects nor stops at three.

input.text: Let me tell you about my visit last week. So, first things first: the hygienist was incredibly gentle and still did the most thorough cleaning I've had in years. Second: they actually run on schedule, I was called back at 2 on the dot. Anyway, that's my story, take it or leave it!

excerpts.text: the hygienist was incredibly gentle and still did the most thorough cleaning I've had in years
they actually run on schedule, I was called back at 2 on the dot

notes: Opening and closing filler ('Let me tell you...', 'Anyway, that's my story...') must not become excerpts; the two aspects hide mid-text behind 'first things first' / 'Second:' lead-ins.

input.text: They told me “you’ll feel a little pinch” and that was genuinely all it was — painless from start to finish. Booking online was easy too, I got a next-day slot without calling anyone.

excerpts.text: They told me “you’ll feel a little pinch” and that was genuinely all it was — painless from start to finish.
Booking online was easy too, I got a next-day slot without calling anyone.

notes: Curly quotes and an em-dash inside the span: a model that normalizes them to ASCII still passes via locateExcerpt's normalized match, but the STORED expected text keeps the original characters.

input.text: La doctora fue muy paciente y me explicó todo el procedimiento en español antes de empezar. La recepción me consiguió una cita el mismo día que llamé.

excerpts.text: La doctora fue muy paciente y me explicó todo el procedimiento en español antes de empezar.
La recepción me consiguió una cita el mismo día que llamé.

notes: Non-English input: two aspects in Spanish, split the same way an English review would be. Accented characters must survive verbatim.

input.text: Absolutely painless wisdom tooth extraction, and the numbness wore off exactly when they said it would.

excerpts.text: Absolutely painless wisdom tooth extraction, and the numbness wore off exactly when they said it would.

notes: One topic, one sentence, just over the 15-word model gate: the whole text is the single excerpt. Guards against inventing a second aspect.

input.text: The dental work itself has been flawless for two years running. Their billing office is the opposite: I was double-charged in March and I'm still chasing the refund.

excerpts.text: The dental work itself has been flawless for two years running.
I was double-charged in March and I'm still chasing the refund.

notes: Positive-care/negative-billing split into exactly two excerpts. The billing excerpt starts mid-sentence past the unquotable 'Their billing office is the opposite:' lead-in.

input.text: Pros: the hygienist is thorough and gentle every single time. Cons: appointments run at least 30 minutes late and nobody warns you.

excerpts.text: the hygienist is thorough and gentle every single time
appointments run at least 30 minutes late and nobody warns you

notes: List-format review: the 'Pros:'/'Cons:' labels are scaffolding, not aspect content — expected spans start after them, though token overlap forgives a model that includes the label.

input.text: I chipped a molar on a Saturday morning and they fit me in before noon the same day. The repair was quick and the tooth has been solid ever since.

excerpts.text: I chipped a molar on a Saturday morning and they fit me in before noon the same day.
The repair was quick and the tooth has been solid ever since.

notes: Emergency-access narrative plus outcome: two aspects even though the story reads as one anecdote.

input.text: My six-year-old actually asks when she gets to go back to the dentist. Dr. Kim turns the whole checkup into a game. The waiting area has books and a fish tank that bought us twenty calm minutes.

excerpts.text: My six-year-old actually asks when she gets to go back to the dentist. Dr. Kim turns the whole checkup into a game.
The waiting area has books and a fish tank that bought us twenty calm minutes.

notes: The first aspect spans TWO sentences that only work together (the second sentence explains the first) — splitting them strands the anecdote. Second aspect is the facility.

input.text: Loved the part where they billed me twice for the same cleaning and called it a system error. To be fair, the hygienist herself was genuinely lovely and careful.

excerpts.text: they billed me twice for the same cleaning and called it a system error
the hygienist herself was genuinely lovely and careful

notes: Sarcastic opener: 'Loved the part where' is not quotable proof, so the expected span starts inside the sentence at the factual clause. The genuine praise still gets its own excerpt.

input.text: ngl i was dreading the root canal but dr patel numbed me up properly and checked in like every five mins and also the front desk printed out exactly what insurance would cover before i sat down which no one has ever done for me

excerpts.text: dr patel numbed me up properly and checked in like every five mins
the front desk printed out exactly what insurance would cover before i sat down

notes: Run-on, lowercase, slangy ('ngl', 'mins') — spans are clauses carved out of one long sentence, copied with the bad grammar intact.

input.text: So this is going to be long, sorry in advance. We switched to this practice when our old dentist retired, which was a whole saga I won't get into, but the point is we were nervous. First visit: Dr. Alvarez spent a full half hour going over my history and x-rays and did not try to upsell me on a single thing, which after our last place felt like a miracle. That said, getting through on the phone is genuinely impossible — I have called four times this month and left messages and the only way to actually reach them is to physically show up. Oh and one more thing, the hygienist gave my teenager a whole gentle lecture about soda that actually landed, he's been brushing twice a day since. Anyway sorry for the essay, overall we're staying.

excerpts.text: Dr. Alvarez spent a full half hour going over my history and x-rays and did not try to upsell me on a single thing
getting through on the phone is genuinely impossible — I have called four times this month and left messages
the hygienist gave my teenager a whole gentle lecture about soda that actually landed

notes: A very long rambling review: three real aspects buried in apology filler, backstory, and 'oh one more thing' asides. None of the filler ('sorry for the essay', the retirement saga) may become an excerpt.

input.text: Waited over an hour with no explanation and no apology. The receptionist talked over me every time I asked a question. Then they pushed a whitening package on me three separate times after I said no.

excerpts.text: Waited over an hour with no explanation and no apology.
The receptionist talked over me every time I asked a question.
they pushed a whitening package on me three separate times after I said no

notes: All-negative multi-topic: three distinct complaints, one excerpt each. Guards against collapsing a rant into a single blob.

input.text: The cleaning was honestly the gentlest I've ever had and also they validated my parking which sounds small but downtown that's real money.

excerpts.text: The cleaning was honestly the gentlest I've ever had
they validated my parking which sounds small but downtown that's real money

notes: One run-on sentence joined by 'and also' holding two unrelated aspects — the split point is mid-sentence, no punctuation to lean on.

input.text: A coworker swore by this place and now I get it. Six months between visits and the front desk still greeted me by name. Dr. Osei remembered which side of my jaw had been bothering me without checking the chart.

excerpts.text: Six months between visits and the front desk still greeted me by name.
Dr. Osei remembered which side of my jaw had been bothering me without checking the chart.

notes: The referral opener ('A coworker swore by this place') is context, not an aspect of the practice's care — it must not become an excerpt.

input.text: Everything is new — they did a 3D scan instead of those gag-inducing impression trays. They also let you pick what plays on the ceiling TV during the cleaning, which made the hour fly by.

excerpts.text: they did a 3D scan instead of those gag-inducing impression trays
They also let you pick what plays on the ceiling TV during the cleaning, which made the hour fly by.

notes: Technology and comfort amenities are separate aspects even though both are 'the office is nice'. Spans start past the unquotable openers ('Everything is new —', 'They also').

input.text: Every single visit, the insurance paperwork is wrong the first time and I have to call and get it resubmitted before my claim will go through.

excerpts.text: Every single visit, the insurance paperwork is wrong the first time and I have to call and get it resubmitted before my claim will go through.

notes: Single-aspect complaint (insurance handling) in one long sentence: exactly one excerpt. Guards against splitting one grievance into fragments.

input.text: Dr. Boone personally called me the evening after my extraction to check how I was healing. Rescheduling my follow-up took one text message.

excerpts.text: Dr. Boone personally called me the evening after my extraction to check how I was healing.
Rescheduling my follow-up took one text message.

notes: Aftercare and scheduling convenience are adjacent but distinct aspects — two excerpts.

input.text: They have actual Saturday hours, which as a teacher is the only reason I can see a dentist at all. And the cleaning itself was careful and unhurried even though I took the last slot of the day.

excerpts.text: They have actual Saturday hours, which as a teacher is the only reason I can see a dentist at all.
the cleaning itself was careful and unhurried even though I took the last slot of the day

notes: Access (weekend hours) versus care quality — two aspects; the second span starts past the sentence-initial 'And'.

input.text: They quoted me a price before the procedure, the final bill matched it to the dollar, and when I asked about a cheaper alternative for the crown they walked me through it instead of pushing the expensive option.

excerpts.text: They quoted me a price before the procedure, the final bill matched it to the dollar, and when I asked about a cheaper alternative for the crown they walked me through it instead of pushing the expensive option.

notes: Every clause is the same aspect (pricing honesty) — one excerpt despite the length and the three-part structure. The over-segmentation trap in billing clothing.

input.text: the denist was very profesional and explaned my xrays in normal words not doctor speak. also there bathroom was spotless which tells you alot about a place

excerpts.text: the denist was very profesional and explaned my xrays in normal words not doctor speak
there bathroom was spotless which tells you alot about a place

notes: Misspellings everywhere ('denist', 'profesional', 'explaned', 'there' for 'their', 'alot') — verbatim means every typo is preserved; a model that fixes even one fails the substring gate.
